response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile is misaligned with this role. His professional C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, with the last decade dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang web/full-stack work. He shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 or SDL practices, MQTT, or industrial protocols, all of which are core to this position. The low-latency DLang systems work is the closest transferable experience, but it does not offset the missing C++ depth and domain expertise for a senior, hands-on C++ architecture role.
strengths: Genuine low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) on high-throughput services at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad polyglot engineering background with strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, open-source test tooling)
Long track record of ownership, mentoring, and knowledge sharing (workshops, courses, founded an open-source platform)
concerns: Modern professional C++/STL experience is minimal and nearly a decade old; primary systems language is DLang, not C++
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, message-broker, or industrial protocol (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) experience
No evidence of Secure Development Lifecycle or IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity practice required by the role

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / MQTT domain experience
